item.title: March Madness Watch Party at Five Iron Golf - Seattle Capitol Hill
item.description:
Thursday, March 26, 2026 at 10 AM

Location: Five Iron Golf - Seattle Capitol Hill, 1525 11th Ave Suite 100, Seattle, WA

Five Iron Golf is your College Basketball HQ this March! Gather your crew and book 1–2 simulator bays for up to 12 people \(6 people per bay\) for a bracket-busting watch party experience. Enjoy all-day TV coverage of college basketball alongside cutting-edge golf and multisport simulators, a locally-inspired menu, and custom beverage packages. Book your watch party for March 19 or 20 and you'll automatically be entered to win a 20-person happy hour with drinks and appetizers — a $1,000 value! Group packages can include reserved event space, team-specific décor, a dedicated staff member, full-screen and/or picture-in-picture viewing, a customizable menu, beverage packages, free club rentals, multisport simulators, and table games. Select the "Small Event" option at booking to ensure simulator bays are placed side-by-side. Availability of select features varies by location. Promotions: Happy Hour – "It's 5i o'Clock Somewhere" \(Monday–Friday, 3–6 PM\) Enjoy $5 bottomless boneless wings, $10 bottomless bone-in wings, $5 select beers/cans/cocktails, $5 dips \(guacamole, hot honey ricotta, hummus\), half-off pizzas, and $5 featured shots. Dine-in only at participating locations; simulator rental may be required. 5i After Dark – "Swing In, For Less"  \(Sunday–Thursday 9 PM to close | Friday–Saturday 10 PM to close\) Features $29/hr simulator rentals plus the same great food and drink specials as Happy Hour, with wings priced per dozen rather than bottomless. Dine-in only at participating locations; simulator rental may be required.

From SEAtoday - Sports & Outdoors

Event details

item.title: 3XBA Spring 3x3 Clinic Series
item.description:
Sunday, March 29, 2026 at 9 AM

Location: The Bush School, 3400 E Harrison St, Seattle, WA

Neighborhoods: Capitol Hill,Central Area/Squire Park,First Hill,Judkins Park,Madison Park,Madrona/Leschi,Montlake/Portage BayEvent Types: Community,Education,Hobbies,Holiday/Seasonal Events, Sports/Recreation,Youth Audience: Children,Special Needs,Teen S ponsoring Organization: 3x3 Basketball Association \(3XBA\) Contact: Lakin Roland Contact Email: info@3xba.com Pre-Register: Yes Cost: $250 \+ fees Registration Details: Two sessions offered each Sunday: Grades 2-5 from 9:00 AM to 11:00 AM, Grades 6-8 from 11:00 AM to 1:00 PM. The clinic runs for five Sundays: March 22, March 29, April 5, Apr il 26, and May 3, 2026 at The Bush School, Seattle. Early bird pricin g ends on March 1. Learn more about 3xBA and register at: https://www.3xba.com/register Link: https://3xba.ludus.com/index.php?show\_id=200517791 Help your athlete grow their game this spring with the 3xBA Spring 3x3 Clinic Series-a 5-week program combining fun, competit ive 3x3 basketball with small-group instruction led by 3xBA coaches. Players develop skills, confidence, and basketball IQ through structured play and hands-on coaching. Open to all experience levels for 2nd-8th graders. Athletes should bring a basketball and water bottle. Perfe ct for off-season development and team-style competition.

From SEAtoday - Sports & Outdoors

item.title: Natasha Zimmers with Naja Lund Aparico - 'Tiny Hiker' and 'Seasons by the Lake: Adventures in Greenl
item.description:
Sunday, March 29, 2026 at 11 AM

Location: Third Place Books Lake Forest Park, 17171 Bothell Way NE,, Lake Forest Park, WA

Welcome spring by celebrating the joy of exploring the great outdoors! Third Place Books welcomes authors Natasha Zimmers and Naja Lund Aparico to our Lake Forest Park store for a storytime featuring Natasha's book Tiny Hikers and Naja's book Seasons by the Lake: Adventures in Greenland. This event is free and open to the public. RSVP is not required. Readers of all ages welcome! About Tiny Hikers . . . "Offers a realistic, joyful depiction of a young adventurer and reflects the author's experience as an outdoor educator. A standout debut that will inspire young readers to explore the outdoors." -- School Library Journal \(SLJ\), starred review The tiny hiker finishes loading up her pack and excitedly gets on the trail, leading her family through the ancient trees toward their beach campsite. At first it's fun to explore--she finds a hollow log, wriggles under a fallen tree, and spots a slimy slug But then the trail starts getting steeper, and when her legs get tired, she trips on a root and takes a hard fall. Now her knee hurts, her elbows hurt...everything hurts She just wants to give up. But after a little break, a tasty snack and an exciting glimpse of the beach, the tiny hiker feels better and decides she will make it. With one final scamper over some giant beach logs, she finds the beach. Time to play And at the end of the long day, after sitting by the campfire, the tiny hiker gets cozy in her sleeping bag and falls fast asleep. About Seasons by the Lake. . . Spend a year experiencing the seasons in Greenland with two Inuit siblings. Brothers Minik and Nuka have a whole year of adventures ahead in Greenland, including finding ice shards from glaciers in the spring to picking crowberries during the summer when the sun barely sets. The darkness of winter reveals Arsarnerit, or the Aurora Borealis, above as the two whisper about the Inuit myth behind the lights. Greenlandic Inuk author Naja Lund Aparico combines a sweet sibling story with an exploration of the seasons in the Arctic featuring stunning illustrations by German and Choctaw illustrator Alex Nees that captures the sweeping landscape. Natasha Zimmers loves being outside in all seasons and weather, but her favourite place to be is on a wild west coast beach, backpacking with her own not-so-tiny hikers. She teaches outdoor education and loves seeing the world through the eyes of her students as they discover the wonders of nature. Naja Lund Aparico is a Greenlandic Inuk author with a background in international relations and currently a second year MFA graduate student at the Institute of American Indian Arts \(IAIA\) with a focus on creative non-fiction. Having grown up in Greenland, she now lives in the Pacific Northwest with her family. Aparico’s children’s books and essays are centered on the indigenous identity with an emphasis on kin, heritage, and the environment. Her US debut picture book, SEASONS BY THE LAKE, will be published by Dial / PRH in March of 2026, while her second picture book \(untitled\) is set to be published by Candlewick in late Spring of 2027, and her third picture book will be published by Dial/PRH in late 2027. Want a signed edition of the featured book, but can't make it to the event? Order through our website or over the phone, and write your request for a signature or personalization in the comments field at checkout. Please call the hosting store if you're placing your order within 24 hours of the event. For media or access inquiries, please email events@thirdplacebooks.com or call our Lake Forest Park store at \(206\) 366-3311.

From SEAtoday - Sports & Outdoors

item.title: Slow Flow Community Yoga
item.description:
Tuesday, March 31, 2026 at 4:30 PM

Tuesday, March 31, 2026, 4:30 - 5:30pm Join us for all-levels slow flow community yoga. Location Link: Greenwood Branch Room Location: Meeting Room Registration Required: Registration is not required. Detailed Description: Yoga instructors Lucia Hunt and Emily Dalgo provide slow flow yoga classes with trauma-informed cueing to make the classes welcoming, accessible, and affirming of all bodies and identities., There will be no expectation of certainyogaexperience to attend classes. Modifications will be offered throughout classes, encouraging people to listen to what feels good in their body and make adjustments as they need and want to. Event Logistics: Yoga mats can be provided at each session., Free parking is available in the Greenwood Branch parking garage and on nearby streets., Patrons joining this class will be asked to complete a liability waiver by the instructors. Contact Us: If you have questions about this event, Ask Us or call 206-386-4636. ADA Accommodations: We can provide accommodations for people with disabilities at Library events. Please contactLEAP at least seven days before the event to request accommodations.,

item.title: LANDSCAPE TECHNICIAN BOOTCAMP
item.description:
Friday, April 3, 2026 at 7 AM

Location: Lake Washington Institute of Technology - LWTech, 11605 132nd Ave NE, Kirkland, WA

LANDSCAPE TECHNICIAN BOOTCAMP Friday, April 3, 2026 Lake Washington Institute of Technology – 11605 132nd Avenue NE, Kirkland, WA 98034-8506 TITLE SPONSOR - DURANTA The Washington Association of Landscape Professionals is excited to offer the LANDSCAPE TECHNICIAN BOOTCAMP training in partnership with the National Association of Landscape Professionals. WALP will be offering the LANDSCAPE TECHNICIAN BOOTCAMP in APRIL 3, 2026 at Lake Washington Insitute of Technology that will be comprehensive training for your staff and workforce. Hands-On Training For Your Crews! The new Landscape Technician Boot Camp will provide hands-on training for your crews. Based on the curriculum for the Landscape Industry Certified Technician Exterior exam, this industry-vetted training will help prepare your crews to work more effectively in 2026. Boot Camp includes training in techniques and safety in 15 different competency areas organized into 3 disciplines presented in Spanish and English! Bonus! This course also helps prepare people for the Landscape Industry Certified Technician Exterior exam. So, once you’ve trained your team, consider registering them to become certified through the National Association of Landscape Professionals \(NALP\). Registration Costs: WALP Members \(First 5 registrants\): $95.00 per person WALP Members \(6\+ registrants\): $80.00 per person NON-WALP Members : $145.00 per person

item.title: Journaling the Garden
item.description:
Saturday, April 4, 2026 at 9 AM

Location: Bellevue Botanical Garden, 12001 Main St, Bellevue, WA

Nature journaling at the Bellevue Botanical Garden is a wonderful way to connect with the environment through curiosity and observation of the natural world. This free adult program will help guide your practice whether you are just starting out or are an experienced journaler. In this warm and welcoming environment, you will be encouraged to experiment and find your voice. In each session we will gather for introductions and brief guidance, and then head out into Garden to journal and come back together to share experiences.   Please bring a dedicated journal and something to write with. We will meet at 9am the first Saturday of January-November 2026. Registration is recommended but not required.  This free adult program is facilitated by Anne Dziok, Adult Education Program Manager. Anne is a practicing multi-media artist who shows her work regionally.    Email us  with questions.

From SEAtoday - Sports & Outdoors

item.title: Eggstravaganza Spring Festival
item.description:
Saturday, April 4, 2026 at 10 AM

Location: Heatherwood Middle School, 1728 142nd Pl SE, Mill Creek, WA

Join the City of Mill Creek for our Eggstravaganza Spring Festival! The event features a continuous egg hunt, where children ages one to eleven can search for eggs that can be exchanged for candy at multiple locations throughout the event. Enjoy photos with the Easter Bunny, family friendly activities and games! Join us for the FREE community event at the Heatherwood Middle School track \(1419 Trillium Blvd. SE, Mill Creek\). Parking is limited, so we encourage you to walk, bike, or carpool.

From SEAtoday - Sports & Outdoors

Event details

item.title: Earth Month Event | Restoration at Gallagher Hill Open Space
item.description:
Saturday, April 4, 2026 at 10 AM

Celebrate Earth Month through Volunteering in your Local Park! Gallagher Hill Open Space is a beautiful park with old trees, a thriving stream, and plenty of native plant and animal life. A lot of the park is unfortunately being overgrown with blackberry and ivy, threating the health of this vital ecosystem. Situated above the freeway Gallagher Hil l is an important respite of nature that is well worth protecting. Come jo in us for a park clean-up where we will be removing ivy. It will be a grea t chance to get your hands in the dirt, learn about native plants and our local forests, and work alongside neighbors and friends! No experience necessary and all ages welcome; gloves and tools are provided. Please note: we require participants aged 14 and under to volunteer with a pare nt or guardian at a ratio of 1 adult to 3 kids. When: Saturday, April 4th, 10am-1pm Where: Gallagher Hill Open Space off of Shorewood Dr, follow VOLUNTEER EVENT Signs What to bring: Dress for the weather in cloth es you don't mind getting dirty \(think: long pants, boots or sneakers\). Bring a water bottle. Small snacks, gloves, and tools will be provided. Registration: https://volunteersignup.org/KMTKY

From SEAtoday - Sports & Outdoors

Event details

item.title: Sailing Shore School
item.description:
Saturday, April 4, 2026 at 10 AM

Location: CWB Boathouse, Lake Union, Seattle, WA

This 2-hour interactive course will introduce you to the basics of sailing. Topics will include terminology for parts of the boat and points of sail, as well as the basic theory of sailing. With the class you will receive the CWB Sailing Primer! Shore School can be taken independently or as part of the SailNOW! or US Sailing Basic Keelboat Certification courses. More information here

From SEAtoday - Sports & Outdoors

item.title: 3XBA Spring 3x3 Clinic Series
item.description:
Sunday, April 5, 2026 at 9 AM

Location: The Bush School, 3400 E Harrison St, Seattle, WA

Neighborhoods: Capitol Hill,Central Area/Squire Park,First Hill,Judkins Park,Madison Park,Madrona/Leschi,Montlake/Portage BayEvent Types: Community,Education,Hobbies,Holiday/Seasonal Events, Sports/Recreation,Youth Audience: Children,Special Needs,Teen S ponsoring Organization: 3x3 Basketball Association \(3XBA\) Contact: Lakin Roland Contact Email: info@3xba.com Pre-Register: Yes Cost: $250 \+ fees Registration Details: Two sessions offered each Sunday: Grades 2-5 from 9:00 AM to 11:00 AM, Grades 6-8 from 11:00 AM to 1:00 PM. The clinic runs for five Sundays: March 22, March 29, April 5, Apr il 26, and May 3, 2026 at The Bush School, Seattle. Early bird pricin g ends on March 1. Learn more about 3xBA and register at: https://www.3xba.com/register Link: https://3xba.ludus.com/index.php?show\_id=200517791 Help your athlete grow their game this spring with the 3xBA Spring 3x3 Clinic Series-a 5-week program combining fun, competit ive 3x3 basketball with small-group instruction led by 3xBA coaches. Players develop skills, confidence, and basketball IQ through structured play and hands-on coaching. Open to all experience levels for 2nd-8th graders. Athletes should bring a basketball and water bottle. Perfe ct for off-season development and team-style competition.

Event details

item.title: March Madness Watch Party at Five Iron Golf - Seattle Capitol Hill
item.description:
Sunday, April 5, 2026 at 10 AM

Location: Five Iron Golf - Seattle Capitol Hill, 1525 11th Ave Suite 100, Seattle, WA

Five Iron Golf is your College Basketball HQ this March! Gather your crew and book 1–2 simulator bays for up to 12 people \(6 people per bay\) for a bracket-busting watch party experience. Enjoy all-day TV coverage of college basketball alongside cutting-edge golf and multisport simulators, a locally-inspired menu, and custom beverage packages. Book your watch party for March 19 or 20 and you'll automatically be entered to win a 20-person happy hour with drinks and appetizers — a $1,000 value! Group packages can include reserved event space, team-specific décor, a dedicated staff member, full-screen and/or picture-in-picture viewing, a customizable menu, beverage packages, free club rentals, multisport simulators, and table games. Select the "Small Event" option at booking to ensure simulator bays are placed side-by-side. Availability of select features varies by location. Promotions: Happy Hour – "It's 5i o'Clock Somewhere" \(Monday–Friday, 3–6 PM\) Enjoy $5 bottomless boneless wings, $10 bottomless bone-in wings, $5 select beers/cans/cocktails, $5 dips \(guacamole, hot honey ricotta, hummus\), half-off pizzas, and $5 featured shots. Dine-in only at participating locations; simulator rental may be required. 5i After Dark – "Swing In, For Less"  \(Sunday–Thursday 9 PM to close | Friday–Saturday 10 PM to close\) Features $29/hr simulator rentals plus the same great food and drink specials as Happy Hour, with wings priced per dozen rather than bottomless. Dine-in only at participating locations; simulator rental may be required.

From SEAtoday - Sports & Outdoors

item.title: Spy Masters Academy
item.description:
Monday, April 6, 2026 at 9:30 AM

Location: Suncatcher Forest School, 18701 se 60th st, Issaquah, WA

Class Experience Calling all young secret agents! This time kids are welcome to join action-packed Outdoor Spy Camp, where kids will train to become top-notch spies through thrilling challenges, daring missions, and teamwork! Obstacle Courses & Ninja Training – Agents-in-training will test their agility, speed, and stealth as they navigate exciting ninja courses, crawl through the forest and learn to use nature objects to complete the challenge. Scavenger Hunts & Riddle Solving – Spies must sharpen their detective skills by decoding secret messages, solving riddles, and uncovering hidden clues to complete their top-secret missions. Nerf Battles & Tactical Challenges – Gear up for friendly Nerf wars, where teamwork and strategy are key to outsmarting the opposition. Agents will also practice their precision and accuracy with target practice and stealth maneuvers. Escape Room-Style Puzzles – Secret agents will work together to crack codes, unlock treasure chests, and solve mind-bending puzzles before time runs out! Outdoor Adventure & Camouflage Training – Spies will blend into their surroundings, practice silent movement, and use nature to their advantage while completing their missions. This high-energy camp is perfect for kids who love adventure, problem-solving, and action-packed fun in the great outdoors! Are you ready to accept the mission? - follow the ticket link to book the camp!

From SEAtoday - Sports & Outdoors

item.title: The Garden w/ Ghost Mountain
item.description:
Monday, April 6, 2026 at 7 PM

Location: Showbox SoDo, 1700 1st Ave. South, Seattle, WA

THE GARDEN IS A CONSTANTLY EVOLVING BAND ORIGINALLY FROM ORANGE COUNTY, CALIFORNIA. ESTABLISHED IN 2011 BY WYATT AND FLETCHER SHEARS. THEY HAVE BEEN TOURING SINCE 2012 AND HAVE PLAYED IN DIFFERENT REGIONS INCLUDING THE UNITED KINGDOM, EUROPE, UNITED STATES, ASIA, AUSTRALIA, MEXICO & CANADA, THEY CREATED THE TERM, "VADA VADA" IN 2011 TO REPRESENT THEIR MUSIC AND OTHER CREATIONS. VADA VADA IS A TERM THAT REPRESENTS TOTAL FREEDOM OF EXPRESSION WITHOUT BOUNDARIES OR GUIDELINES OF ANY SORT. THEY HAVE BECOME KNOWN FOR THEIR ENERGETIC AND OFF THE WALL PERFORMANCES AROUND THE WORLD, AND ARE PROLIFICALLY TOURING, RELEASING MUSIC AND OTHER CONTENT.

item.title: Earth Month Event | Mindfulness Walk
item.description:
Tuesday, April 7, 2026 at 11 AM

Celebrate Earth Month with a Mindfulness Walk in the Park! Taking inspiration from the Japanese idea of shinrin-yoku \(forest bathin g\), we will be hosting a series of mindfulness walks in Pioneer Park. Mindfulness walking in the forest has shown to have a myriad of benefits from improving heart health to reducing stress \(https://www.npr.org/2023/08/22 /1195337204/a-guide-to-forest-bathing\). Forest Steward Marilyn Cornwe ll \(https://www.soulscapes.us/\) will be leading an hour-long guided walk t hrough the park. Please join us if your curious about mindfulness, want to learn more about and experience healing powers of being in nature, and/or if you want to deepen your connection with Pioneer Park and others. When: Tuesday, April 4th, 11am-12pm Where: Pioneer Park NW, Meet at the corner of Island Crest Way and SE 68th St. Follow VOLUNTEER EVENT Signs What to bring: Dress for the weather in clothes and shoes you will comfo rtable walking and standing in, as well as water.

Event details

item.title: Ratboys
item.description:
Tuesday, April 7, 2026 at 7 PM

Location: Neumos, 925 E Pike St, Seattle, WA

Neumos Presents When The Sun Explodes Tour RATBOYS villagerrr TUE, 7 APR 2026 at 07:00PM PDT Ages: 21 & Over Doors Open: 07:00PM OnSale: Fri, 26 Sep 2025 at 07:00AM PDT Announcement: Tue, 23 Sep 2025 at 07:00AM PDT Ratboys present a momentous new single, “Light Night Mountains All That,” and announce a North American tour. This is the band’s first new music, and their first with New West Records, since The Window, one of the most-praised albums of 2023. The Window catapulted the band from being beloved to “feverishly fixated upon” \(Stereogum\), and “Light Night Mountains All That” seizes that attention and doesn’t let it look away. The Window received a wealth of critical praise from the likes of The New York Times, NPR Music, Rolling Stone, Billboard, Stereogum, and Uproxx, while Pitchfork called it "a level-up record" for the band. “Light Night Mountains All That” is singer/guitarist Julia Steiner’s most thrilling vocal take yet, bolstered by a powerhouse performance by guitarist Dave Sagan, drummer Marcus Nuccio, bassist Sean Neumann, and piano by track co-producer Chris Walla. The track, deemed their “wormhole jam” by Steiner, is driven by an unorthodox time structure, exhilarating instrumentation, and the flare of extraterrestrial guitar bloops. In early 2026, Ratboys will perform across North America. As Paste praises, Ratboys have “a live chemistry that remains unparalleled and irreplaceable.” Important Information: \* This event is 21 & over. Valid ID is required. Minors will not be permitted entry. \* Doors open at the listed event time. \* Event start time is generally one hour after doors open but is subject to change without notice. \* All tickets are nonrefundable with the exception of event cancellation. \* Support acts are subject to change. \* Orders in violation of the published ticket limit are subject to cancellation without notice.

item.title: Tai Chi for Health and Balance
item.description:
Wednesday, April 8, 2026 at 6 PM

Location: Seattle Central College Continuing Education, 1701 Broadway, Seattle, WA

Class Overview Tai Chi is a health sustaining exercise and a tool for improved body awareness. Practiced by the elderly for balance and circulation and the young for relaxation and martial arts, it is a wonderful holistic activity for all ages. We'll focus on learning the entire Yang short form, and you'll receive guidance in developing your own regular practice at home. During this class, you'll learn: -The Yang Tai Chi short form, which is comprised of 35 individual movements -Chi Kung, a warm-up exercise and relaxation activity with 18 movements -Elements of Chinese Medical Philosophy, such as Yin/Yang theory and Five Elements transformation -the basics of Taoist philosophy Learning Objectives Learn basic warm-up stretches to improve your overall movement Discover the Yang-style short form of Tai Chi Practice the Yang-style under the direction of an expert instructor Gain knowledge about the history and background of Tai Chi Who Might Enjoy This Class? Individuals seeking a low-impact exercise routine Anyone interested in improving their coordination and balance People interested in developing their focus, relaxation and overall wellness

item.title: Stifel Stars on Ice Pre-Show Stargazer Pass
item.description:
Saturday, May 23, 2026 at 7 PM

Location: Climate Pledge Arena, 334 1st Ave N, Seattle, WA

THIS IS AN OPTIONAL UPGRADE. TICKET TO THE MAIN EVENT IS ALSO REQUIRED. Pre-Show Stargazer Event takes place prior to Stars on Ice show. Enjoy early entry, watch the skater warm-ups, and participate in a Q&A with several skaters from specified seating sections. Due to this event taking place prior to doors opening, EVERYONE entering the venue early must have an event ticket AND Pre-Show Stargazer Pass for entry. This includes all children as well as the parent(s)/guardian(s) wishing to accompany them into the building. If you plan on attending the Pre-Show Stargazer Event, please make sure you have BOTH tickets. NO EXCEPTIONS. Late arrivals will not be refunded. OPTIONAL UPSELL. NOT VALID FOR EVENT ADMISSION. MUST ALSO HAVE EVENT TICKET! FAN MUST BRING UPSELL TICKET TO EVENT DAY FOR ENTRY. NOTE SPECIFIC PRE-SHOW ACCESS START TIME! STARGAZER PASS TICKET REQUIRED FOR ENTRY.

From Climate Pledge Arena
